mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held below its recent cycle peak, equity discount rates ease and the S&P 500 should rise over the next month. If rate cuts do not coincide with higher 20-day returns, the mechanism is false.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in most periods with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the hit rate is not above 50% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The effect should appear after the unemployment rate crosses higher by at least 0.3 percentage points within three months.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is accelerating while HOUST is also firm, the market reads sustained consumption and construction demand as supporting earnings, so the S&P 500 should trend higher over the next quarter. The signal fails if both series weaken or if retail sales growth is offset by housing contraction.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone in explaining 60-day returns, or if positive outcomes do not exceed 55% when both series are above trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage, which should lift equity prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when production is expanding after a prior slowdown and weakens if output growth stalls.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with INDPRO above its 3-month average do not show materially higher 20-day SPX returns than periods below the average.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior peak signals tightening interest-sensitive demand and slower cyclical growth, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be visible once housing starts fall below their recent 6-month trend.
failure_condition: Rejected if downward-trend housing starts are followed by positive SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation running above trend while FEDFUNDS is still high implies tighter real policy and lower equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month. The mechanism is strongest when CPIAUCSL is accelerating and policy has not yet eased.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month trend and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Rejected if high-inflation/high-rate trigger episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power relative to the single series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A broad payroll decline in PAYEMS together with rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not active unless employment weakens while unemployment is simultaneously increasing.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines over 3 months and UNRATE rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint labor-deterioration condition does not generate negative 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or if one proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP level in GDPC1 combined with expanding RSAFS indicates resilient aggregate demand that supports earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim fails when demand strength is not broad-based across output and sales.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with concurrent GDP and retail sales strength do not show higher 60-day SPX returns than periods without joint strength, or if the conditional alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
